_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. This SWMS applies any time workers are on site with water in the pool — finishing trades, snagging, handover, return visits for warranty work.

activity_name: Working on site with water in the pool (snagging, finishing, return visits)

hazards.title: Drowning — fall into pool
hazards.risk: Worker fall into pool from coping / surrounds; especially in wet conditions; especially with tools / equipment causing instability

controls.title: Where possible, schedule work that requires close-to-edge work BEFORE pool is filled
Pool safety barrier remains closed except for active access; never propped open
No solo work within 2m of pool edge
At least one worker on site with current first-aid + CPR

hazards.title: Electric shock — tools near water
hazards.risk: Power tools / extension leads near pool water

controls.title: Battery-powered tools only within 2m of pool water
Any mains-powered tool used near pool must be on RCD-protected circuit; cords kept out of water

hazards.title: Existing pool chemicals on site
hazards.risk: Stored balancing chemicals owned by client; worker disturbing storage

controls.title: Do not handle owner's chemical store; if access required, confirm with owner

hazards.title: Slips on wet coping / paving
hazards.risk: Wet surfaces around pool; especially after rain or pool use

tasks: Pre-start: confirm pool barrier compliance; confirm barrier closed and latching properly.
Toolbox: review drowning risk; confirm buddy system within 2m of pool edge.
Confirm at least one current first-aid / CPR ticket on site.
Use battery tools only within 2m of pool water; mains tools on RCD circuit.
Keep all cords, hoses, fluids away from pool edge.
No glass containers within pool surrounds.
Re-close barrier whenever access path not actively in use.
Photograph site state on departure; confirm barrier closed and latched.
